Title: Co-CEO AI Agent for EOS-Aligned Leadership

The Challenge:
Case Study Summary:


Veracity Consulting, an IT services firm operating on the Entrepreneurial Operating System (EOS), set out to build an intelligent Co-CEO assistant for its founder, Angela Hurt. The vision: an AI partner that could answer internal questions, route requests to the right leader, and surface structured decision support in real time.


Halfway through the project, the team is focused on the foundation—building the data pipeline and training specialized AI agents to “think” like the company’s key influencers. By modeling the decision-making principles, leadership profiles, and operating rhythms of Veracity’s leadership team, the assistant is being shaped into a trusted counterpart. 


The goal is not just efficiency, but to provide data-driven clarity that balances the founder’s natural people-first, intuition-driven style with structured, evidence-based insight



The Challenge:


Veracity needed a way to bring more structure and clarity to decision making.

  • Often when key decisions arise, the right data isn’t available or connected

  • Input from the team tends toward consensus, not clear recommendations

  • Leading indicators aren’t consistently tied to long-term revenue outcomes

  • Important context (values, culture, performance patterns) lives in people’s heads, not systems


Our Approach:


We focused on building the foundation for an intelligent Co-CEO assistant that could deliver structured decision support in real time.

  • Designed a data pipeline to unify leadership profiles, EOS tools, and business metrics

  • Trained AI agents to “think” like Veracity’s key influencers and domain leaders

  • Embedded cultural values and decision-making principles into the system logic

  • Structured outputs to surface insights, route requests, and recommend next steps with clarity



The Result:


Halfway through the project, the Co-CEO assistant is already reshaping how Veracity captures and uses knowledge.

  • Weekly leadership meetings are recorded and saved into the database, giving Angi on-demand access to review notes

  • Prototype agents provide structured summaries that highlight key themes and action items

  • EOS tools, leadership profiles, and financial goals are being connected into a unified data foundation

  • Early outputs show clearer visibility into leading indicators and how they tie to long-term revenue targets

Short Description:
AI-Powered Quality Data Platform for Smarter Textile Manufacturing


Kyungbang’s quality control operations were transformed by an AI platform that parses, standardizes, and analyzes fiber test data across suppliers and factory machines.

→ PDF parsing, anomaly flagging, and natural language data search
Title: AI-Powered Quality Platform for Global Textile Manufacturing
Subtitle: Standardized Fiber Data, Real-Time Insights, and Smarter Production Decisions
The Challenge:
Case Study Summary:


Kyungbang, a global textile manufacturer, manages one of the most complex challenges in its industry: high-volume fiber testing across suppliers and factories worldwide. Data arrived in scattered formats—PDFs, Excel sheets, raw text—forcing engineers to spend hours reconciling numbers before making decisions.


Edge8 partnered with Kyungbang to build an AI-powered quality platform designed to unify that data, flag anomalies in real time, and allow engineers to query results in natural language. The system is becoming the foundation for faster, more confident decisions on the factory floor—and a launchpad for predictive blending and other future AI enhancements.



The Challenge:


Kyungbang’s quality control was slowed by manual, offline workflows that made fast decisions nearly impossible. Eight team members walked daily to more than 100 testing machines that weren’t connected online. Each day, they collected results on drives, keyed them into spreadsheets, and compiled thick printed books for the Director to review. The process left little room for agility when quality issues arose.

  • Supplier and factory data scattered across inconsistent formats (PDF, Excel, text)

  • Manual entry of results into spreadsheets, then printed for review

  • No direct connectivity from testing machines to decision-makers

  • Engineers lacked intuitive tools to quickly query or compare test outcomes



Our Approach:


Edge8 worked with Kyungbang to replace manual collection and reconciliation with a unified AI-powered quality platform. The goal was not only speed, but to give engineers and leaders confidence in the insights driving production choices.

  • Built an AI-driven document parsing engine to ingest QC reports from any format

  • Developed a system for real-time anomaly detection and automated flagging

  • Enabled natural language querying for batch, machine, and location histories

  • Integrated the system directly into existing QC workflows across facilities



The Result:


The new platform has already reshaped Kyungbang’s quality operations, cutting waste and speeding up decisions that directly impact production efficiency.

  • Reduced manual reconciliation time by over 50%

  • Engineers retrieve insights instantly using everyday language instead of spreadsheets

  • Faster, more confident production decisions across multiple facilities

  • Established a scalable foundation for predictive blending and future AI-driven enhancements
SEO Title Tag: AI-Powered Data Platform for Textile Innovation | Edge8 Case Study
SEO Meta Description: Discover how Edge8 delivered a digital platform for Kyungbang, leveraging AI to standardize fiber data and improve global manufacturing intelligence.
